title: Taxi

shortDialogue.speaker: 우정
shortDialogue.english: I will pay with a transportation card.

longDialogue.speaker: 우정
longDialogue.english: Oh, I don't know the routes well.

longDialogue.speaker: 택시 기사
longDialogue.english: Seong-san Bridge has a lot of traffic now.

longDialogue.speaker: 우정
longDialogue.korean: 그래요? 지금 늦었어요. 그래서 빨리 가야 돼요.
longDialogue.english: Really? I'm late now. So, I have to get there quickly.

longDialogue.speaker: 택시 기사
longDialogue.english: Alright. Shall I go to the entrance of Gyeong-bok Palace?

longDialogue.speaker: 우정
longDialogue.english: Yes, let me out at the entrance.

longDialogue.speaker: 우정
longDialogue.english: I will pay with a transportation card.

culturalTip: Taxi drivers in Korea are notorious for denying passengers based on their destination, especially if you try to take a taxi at night in a busy area. Some taxi drivers want to take passengers whose destination is far away, while others choose to only take passengers to a destination within a few minutes of the starting point. Nowadays, you can use a mobile app to summon a taxi by entering your current location and your destination. Sometimes, however, your request may not be accepted quickly if you try to use the app at night in a busy area.
koreanReview:
- Short Dialogue
택시 기사: 다 왔습니다.
우정: 교통 카드로 할게요.

- Long Dialogue
택시 기사: 어디 가세요?
우정: 경복궁이요.
택시 기사: 어느 쪽으로 갈까요?
우정: 아, 저 길 잘 몰라요.
택시 기사: 성산대교는 지금 엄청 막혀요.
우정: 그래요? 지금 늦었어요. 그래서 빨리 가야 돼요.
택시 기사: 그럼 양화대교로 갈게요.
우정: 네, 빨리 좀 가 주세요.
택시 기사: 알겠습니다.
택시 기사: 경복궁 입구로 갈까요?
우정: 네, 입구에서 내려 주세요.
택시 기사: 다 왔습니다.
우정: 교통 카드로 할게요.
택시 기사: 네, 안녕히 가세요.

extendedVocab.meaning: to have a lot of traffic

grammar.title: Inquiring
grammar.pattern: VERB + -시- + -아/어/여요? = Do you VERB? / Are you VERB-ing? (honorific)
grammar.meaning: Do you VERB? / Are you VERB-ing?
grammar.explanation: * When -시- is combined with -아/어/여요, it becomes -세요. Over time however, it changed to -세요 because it is easier to pronounce.

examples.english: 가세요? = Do you go? / Are you going?

exercises.english: Do you come? / Are you coming?
Do you watch? / Are you watching?

grammar.title: Present Perfect - Accomplishment
grammar.pattern: 다 + VERB + -았/었/였습니다 = I have finished VERB-ing. / I have VERB-ed all. (polite, formal)
grammar.meaning: I have finished VERB-ing. / I have VERB-ed all.

examples.english: 다 왔습니다. = I have come all the way. / We are here.

pronunciation.rule: When a syllable ends with ㄹ and is followed by a syllable that begins with ㄱ, the ㄱ is pronounced as ㅇ.
Let's review a few pronunciation rules that have been covered already.
